Frequently asked questions

Everything about how cross-referencing works and when online search is used.

Enter a filter part number and we'll find every equivalent across all major brands. The tool uses the curated catalog database as its primary source and can optionally expand to online search with your consent.

It's optional. Part numbers are often reused across manufacturers — the same digits can mean different filters from different brands. Specifying your current brand disambiguates and returns the exact equivalent.

The catalog database covers brands and part numbers published in the cross-reference catalog. Parts that are newly released, highly specialized, or entered with a typo may not be listed. In those cases, we offer an optional online search.

Never automatically. The catalog database is always searched first and remains the highest authority. If no match is found there, we ask your explicit permission before expanding to online sources. Online results are clearly marked as external and flagged for verification.

Catalog matches reflect the equivalences published in the official cross-reference catalog and are the most reliable source. Online results, when used, are a starting point and should always be verified against the manufacturer's official specifications.

Expand search to online sources?

No match was found in the catalog database. Would you like us to perform an additional online search for possible matches?

Online results are marked separately and should be verified against the manufacturer's official specifications.

Online search settings

Online search uses the Anthropic API. To enable it on a hosted version of this tool, paste your Anthropic API key below. The key is stored only in your browser (localStorage) and sent directly to Anthropic — it never touches any other server.

No API key configured
Get a key from console.anthropic.com. API calls are billed to your Anthropic account. Storing an API key in a browser is only appropriate for personal or internal team tools — for public-facing deployments use a backend proxy instead.
Copied
